title: elymus farctus subsp. bessarabicus
intro: Elymus farctus subsp. bessarabicus, also known as Bessarabian wheat grass, is a subspecies of a type of grass native to Eastern Europe and Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Elymus farctus subsp. bessarabicus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to create salves and creams for topical application.
